summary: crypto-credential-scanner exfiltrates host data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint via install hook
details: The package's package.json declares a lifecycle script that uses child_process, reads process.env, gathers hostname/identity, and POSTs to https://ddjidd564.github.io. The bundled scanner.js (referenced from the lifecycle hook) imports fs, os, https, and child_process; calls execSync('whoami'), os.hostname(), and walks the filesystem with fs.existsSync; then sends the collected system intelligence to the same hardcoded https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint via https.request/https.get and curl. This is a textbook host-fingerprint + credential-scanning exfiltration payload reaching an attacker-controlled GitHub Pages domain. The package name ("crypto-credential-scanner") and the code's evident purpose of enumerating credentials/files on the installer's machine make the malicious intent unambiguous. Installing this package causes the installer's hostname, username, working directory, environment data, and filesystem reconnaissance to be shipped off-host to an attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
